ab: Ye malu meyah ku smiling isu?
en: A:Can I interview you?

ab: Malu, smruwa ku.
en: B: Yes, I would love to.

ab: Ye malu meyah ku smiling isu?
en: A:Can I interview you?

ab: Iya, ini kegu gnklaun mu.
en: B:Don’t, I don’t know much about it.

ab: Ye niqan ali su bbiyan? Mkmusa ku smiling sunan, malu ho?
ch: A:你下午有空嗎?我想去拜訪你可以嗎?
en: A: Are you free this afternoon? I’d like to visit you, is that okay?

ab: Malu, traun sumu sapah mu.
en: B: Okay, I’ll wait for you at home.

ab: Niqan ali su kusun? Mkmeyah ku smiling so bnhang su Seediq.
ch: A:你明天有空嗎?我想請問你關於賽德克族的傳說故事。
en: A: Are you free tomorrow? I’d like to ask you about the saga of the Sedecks.

ab: Maha ku dgiyaq, ini ku eniq sapah.
ch: B:明天我要去山上，不在家喔。
en: B: I’m going to the mountains tomorrow, so I won’t be home.

ab: Klaun su ba gaga Seediq mesa bnhangan mu, mkmeyah ku ptgesa isu.
ch: A:我聽說您對賽德克族的傳統習俗非常了解，想要請教您一下。
en: A: I’ve heard that you’re very knowledgeable about the traditional customs of the Sedykes and would like to ask you a few questions.

ab: Ini keegu gnklaan mu, kiya ge tlame mu cmiyuk rmengo.
ch: B:我所了解的不多，但是我會盡量回答。
en: B: I don’t know much about it, but I will try to answer as best I can.

ab: Ye ku malu psasing? Kiya maha leexan spatis.
ch: A:請問我可以錄影嗎?這樣以後比較容易記錄。
en: A: Can I record the video? It will be easier to record in the future.

ab: Malu, ini huwa.
en: B: Okay, no problem.

ab: Piya kngkawas su kawas saya di?
en: A: How old are you?

ab: Dehu ku 98 kngkawas di.
en: B: I’m 98 years old.

ab: Ma su wakayi na.
en: A: You’re so young.

ab: Ma su riso na.
en: A: You’re so young.

ab: Ma su weewa na.
en: A: You’re so young.

ab: Uxe wa! Ruda ku di.
en: B: No, I’m not! I’m old.

ab: Pneyah su inu ho?
en: A: Where are you from?

ab: Pneyah ku Tongan.
en: B: I’m from Meixi.

ab: Ye seediq uri ka qedil su?
ch: A:請問您的太太也是賽德克族人嗎?
en: A: Is your wife also a Sedek?

ab: Uxe, umBgala ka heya.
en: B: No, she’s a umBgala person.

ab: So kari mmaanu klaun su rmengo?
en: A: What languages do you speak?

ab: Mkela ku rmengo kari Sbnawan ma kari Qnhaqun.
en: B: I speak Sbnawan and Qnhaqun.

ab: Mqaras ku dehuk ku meyah smiling sunan, rengo tikoh, so gaya musa tmlenga ho?
ch: A:今天很高興能訪問您，請您談一下，有關狩獵的禁忌好嗎?
en: A: It’s a pleasure to interview you today, can you talk about the taboo of hunting?

ab: Un, slaqi ku ciida we musa ku paha tlenga camac.
ch: B:好的。我年輕的時候很常上山打獵。
en: B: Yes. I used to go hunting in the mountains when I was young.

ab: Mhuwe su, malu ba tnugesa su ali saya.
ch: A:謝謝，您今天所教導的真是太棒了。
en: A: Thank you, what you taught me today was really great.

ab: Bable ba kana rnengo mu, snneru na rudan cbeyo.
ch: B:我所説的都是千真萬確的，祖先所流傳的講述。
en: B: All that I say is true, the sayings of my ancestors.

ab: Betaq hini han sniling mu, mhuwe su bale bnege su.
ch: A:今天的訪談就到這邊。非常謝謝您的分享。
en: A: That’s all for today’s interview. Thank you very much for sharing with us.

ab: Iya qsiqa, prengo ta bobo na di.
en: B: You’re welcome, let’s talk again.

ab: Maha ku han. Psmeyah ku egu srengo su kari ndaan.
ch: A:我要離開了，期待下次聽到更多的故事。
en: A: I’m going to leave now and look forward to hearing more stories next time.

ab: Malu wa, knhuwe musa di ha.
en: B: Okay! Please take care.
